user_query:
Act as my executive assistant for this week. Read all of the following files under input/ and then produce the requested artifacts under output/:

Inputs to read:
- input/boss_profile.md — my identity and communication style
- input/secretary_memory.md — current memory snapshot (you will update this)
- input/current_priorities.txt — explicit scheduling rules/preferences to add to memory
- input/calendar_week.json — my current week’s events and focus blocks (Europe/Madrid)
- input/inbox.jsonl — five incoming items that need replies and/or scheduling decisions

Deliverables to create:
1) Draft replies (do not send):
   - Write one draft per item in input/inbox.jsonl in my voice and tone from boss_profile.md.
   - Each draft must begin with a Subject: line and include a concise body ready for my review.
   - Save each as a separate file under output/drafts/ named email_1.md, email_2.md, etc. (five total).
   - Ensure the drafts reflect my preferences: default 30-minute meetings, push back on 60-minute requests, protect focus time, and be warm-professional and concise. For vendors, politely decline or defer. For VIP investors, accept but propose a compliant slot. For internal reschedules, propose practical alternatives.

2) Proposed calendar actions (suggestions only — require my confirmation):
   - Based on inbox items and calendar_week.json, propose a set of actions that respect the explicit rules in input/current_priorities.txt and avoid conflicts.
   - Include reschedules and/or declines where appropriate (e.g., no Monday morning meetings, 15-minute buffers, mornings until 10 are focus, Friday afternoons no external meetings, add 30 minutes of prep before important meetings, default 30 minutes unless truly needed longer, VIP investor calls accepted soon but compliant with rules).
   - Write a machine-readable plan to output/calendar_actions.json as an array of objects with fields: {"action":"schedule|reschedule|decline|hold","title":"string","requested_by":"string","proposed_time":{"start":"ISO8601","duration_min":number}|null,"reason":"string","requires_confirmation":true}. Include at least one decline and at least one reschedule.

3) One-page meeting brief:
   - Prepare a brief for the board meeting request in the inbox (or the week’s board meeting if applicable). Include the sections: "Attendee brief", "Context", "Logistics", and "Talking points".
   - Save to output/briefs/brief_board_meeting.md.

4) Updated memory and rules (text files only):
   - Produce a consolidated memory file under output/secretary/memory.md (≤100 lines) that keeps a lean snapshot of my identity, voice, key rules, and an Index pointing to people.md and calendar.md. Update only with explicit items from input/current_priorities.txt; do not infer additional preferences from emails.
   - Create output/secretary/calendar.md capturing scheduling rules: protected time (mornings until 10), Friday afternoons no external meetings, 15-minute buffers, 30-minute default length, 30-minute prep for important meetings, and work hours.
   - Create output/secretary/people.md with brief relationship notes for at least Maria Chen (Investor) and David (CTO) so future scheduling can reference them.

Important constraints:
- Do not send anything; drafts and action plans are for my confirmation only.
- Respect my explicit rules from current_priorities.txt and do not auto-learn beyond them.
- Use Europe/Madrid times. Keep clarity and brevity. Ensure every proposed calendar action includes requires_confirmation: true.

Paths to write:
- output/drafts/email_1.md ... email_5.md
- output/calendar_actions.json
- output/briefs/brief_board_meeting.md
- output/secretary/memory.md
- output/secretary/calendar.md
- output/secretary/people.md
